description: Synthetic behavioral-risk and harmless-control examples. These cases test whether ShieldVault offers a useful pause without claiming certainty about intent.

cases.text: You are completely incompetent and everyone here knows it.
cases.reason: Direct personal attack in a public professional context deserves a second look, but the user retains agency.

cases.text: Keep pushing me and you are going to regret it.
cases.context: Public social-media reply during an argument.
cases.reason: The wording can be understood as a threat and should prompt reconsideration without asserting criminal intent.

cases.text: Thanks for finally doing the bare minimum.
cases.context: Comment on a colleague's public post.
cases.reason: Likely passive aggression, though tone can depend on relationship and context.

cases.text: Really impressive how professionally you handled that.
cases.context: Reply during an active workplace dispute.
cases.reason: The sentence may be sincere or sarcastic. ShieldVault must not pretend to know which.

cases.text: Since everybody apparently knows anyway, I may as well explain what happened with my sister's divorce.
cases.reason: The post proposes disclosing another person's private situation in public.

cases.text: I am done being quiet. Tonight I am naming every person who screwed me over.
cases.reason: Signals an emotionally charged, potentially irreversible public disclosure.

cases.text: This company steals from its employees and the manager knows exactly what he is doing.
cases.context: Public review written while still employed.
cases.reason: A serious public accusation may carry professional and legal consequences and deserves a deliberate pause.

cases.text: I hope everyone gets exactly what they deserve.
cases.context: Standalone social post after a dispute.
cases.reason: The same wording can be warm, ominous, or joking depending on context.

cases.text: I HAVE ASKED THREE TIMES. STOP IGNORING ME.
cases.reason: All-caps and imperative phrasing indicate elevated intensity, but may be justified by context.

cases.text: For anyone wondering, yes, we broke up, and yes, I can finally tell you why.
cases.reason: Invites an emotionally charged disclosure involving another person.

cases.text: This restaurant was insanely good and the staff absolutely crushed it.
cases.reason: Strong language is clearly positive and should not be mistaken for aggression.

cases.text: The character says, 'You are completely incompetent,' but the scene is played for comedy.
cases.reason: Hostile wording is quoted for analysis rather than directed at the recipient.

cases.text: I am not available for this conversation tonight. We can revisit it tomorrow.
cases.reason: Firm boundary-setting is not inherently hostile or regrettable.

cases.text: I disagree with your conclusion, but I understand how you got there.
cases.reason: Clear disagreement expressed respectfully should not trigger a behavioral warning.

cases.text: STOP USING THE MACHINE — THE GUARD IS BROKEN.
cases.reason: Intensity and all-caps are justified by immediate safety context.

cases.text: I am angry right now, so I am going to wait before posting the rest of this.
cases.context: Public draft that explicitly chooses restraint.
cases.reason: The writer is already exercising deliberate self-control; warning again may be needless friction.
